title: Using Mastery Goals for personalized practice
description: A check for understanding of the Using course mastery on Khan Academy lesson.
topic_path: Discover Mastery Goals on Khan Academy
Using Mastery Goals for personalized practice

assessment_items.question_content:
**As a teacher, where would you find the current Mastery Goals for the students in your class?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Teachers can create, view and edit course mastery goals from the **progress tab** on the Class dashboard.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the image below. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c516f228935b77cf3188d473b20d1748.png)

**As a teacher, where would you go to edit a mastery goal for your students?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Teachers can create, view, and edit course mastery goals from the **progress tab** on the Class dashboard.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the image below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**As a teacher, where would you go to create a mastery goal for your students?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Teachers can create and edit course mastery goals from the **mastery goals: assign tab** on the Class dashboard.

options.alt: Image of a computer screen. The top says "teacher dashboard". Below that is the heading "Assign course or unit mastery". There are 3 columns below that: Select course, Select content, and Set due date.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of Khan Academy's course mastery system.

**Label each skill level.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]
hints:
Below is the image of the correct answers.

[[☃ image 1]]

options.imageAlt: Skill levels in Khan Academy's course mastery system

markers.label: The first dot on the image. Below it says, "students got fewer than 70% correct on an exercise."
The third dot from the top. Below, it says, "students got 100% correct on an exercise."
The second dot from the top. Below it says, "Students got 70-99% correct on an exercise."
The fourth point from the top. It says, "Students were at proficient and correctly answered questions on this skill on a mixed-skill assessment."
Last bullet point. Below it says, "students haven't worked on this skill yet".

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the table with the mastery skill level that matches appropriate skill requirement.**

Color | Skill level | Requirements
- | - | -
[[☃ image 1]] | [[☃ dropdown 1]] | Student answers fewer than $70\%$ of the questions correctly on a practice exercise.
[[☃ image 3]] | [[☃ dropdown 2]] | Student answers $70\%-99\%$ of the questions correctly on a practice exercise.
[[☃ image 5]] | [[☃ dropdown 3]] | Student answers $100\%$ of the questions correctly on a practice exercise.
[[☃ image 7]]| [[☃ dropdown 4]] | Student **who is already at the proficient mastery level** correctly answers a question on a unit test, course challenge, or mastery challenge.
hints:
Here are the correct skill levels:

Color| Skill level | Requirements
- | - | -
[[☃ image 2]] | **Attempted**| Students got fewer than $70\%$ of the questions correct on an exercise.
[[☃ image 3]] | **Familiar** | Students completed an exercise with $70\%-99\%$ of the questions correct.
[[☃ image 4]] |  **Proficient** | Students got $100\%$ of the questions correct.
[[☃ image 5]]| **Mastered** | Students were already at the proficient mastery level **and** correctly answered a question on a unit test, course challenge, or mastery challenge.

options.alt: The outline of a red-orange square with approximately  one-sixth filled in.
The outline of an orange square with approximately  one-half  filled in.
A light purple square fully shaded in.
A dark purple square fully shaded in with a white crown in the middle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank.**

In Khan Academy's mastery system, when students answer questions correctly their mastery level [[☃ dropdown 1]], and when they answer questions incorrectly their mastery level  [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: In Khan Academy's mastery system, when students answer questions correctly their mastery level **moves up**, and when they answer questions incorrectly their mastery level **moves down**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following can a student use to demonstrate skill mastery (i.e. reach the Mastered level) on Khan Academy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Unit tests**, **course challenges** and **mastery challenges** are mixed skill practice that can be used to demonstrate skill mastery on Khan Academy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank.**

[[☃ dropdown 1]] are a way for students to review and practice previously learned skills in a course in just 6 questions.
hints: **Mastery challenges** are a way for students to review and practice previously learned skills in a course in just 6 questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blank.** 

In a [[☃ dropdown 1]] learning environment, there is not an expectation that the teacher be the gatekeeper to all knowledge or skills.
hints: In a **mastery** learning environment, there is not an expectation that the teacher be the gatekeeper to all knowledge or skills.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.** 

In a mastery learning classroom, the teacher should provide students with regular, meaningful [[☃ dropdown 1]] on skill [[☃ dropdown 2]] and provide suggested resources for improving skill practice and progress.
hints: In a mastery learning classroom, the teacher should provide students with regular, meaningful **feedback** on skill **progress** and provide suggested resources for improving skill practice and progress.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

A first step to transitioning to mastery learning using Khan Academy is to set a [[☃ dropdown 1]] mastery goal for students, such as setting a course mastery goal to earn [[☃ dropdown 2]] or greater mastery in Algebra I, and support student progress towards their mastery goal.
hints: A first step to transitioning to mastery learning using Khan Academy is to set a **long-term** mastery goal for students, such as setting a course mastery goal to earn **80%** or greater mastery in Algebra I, and support student progress towards their mastery goal.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

Many teachers in [[☃ dropdown 1]] classrooms encourage integrating  [[☃ dropdown 2]] activities to help students better embrace the idea that they are capable of learning.
hints: Many teachers in **mastery** classrooms encourage integrating **growth mindset** activities to help students better embrace the idea that they are capable of learning.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

One of the key messages to share with students is [[☃ dropdown 1]] learning focuses on what they [[☃ dropdown 3]]  need to be successful and [[☃ dropdown 4]]  understand concepts, rather than just moving through concepts whether they really understand them or not.
hints: One of the key messages to share with students is **mastery** learning focuses on what they **individually** need to be successful and **deeply** understand concepts, rather than just moving through concepts whether they really understand them or not.
